rules.title: Gray Water Tank Requirement
rules.description: Any mobile food unit with open food preparation must have a pressurized gray water holding tank of at least 15 gallons capacity, with a shut-off valve.

rules.title: Commissary Kitchen Agreement
rules.description: All mobile food units must have a signed agreement with an approved commissary kitchen for daily cleaning, food storage, and waste disposal.

rules.title: Food Handler Certification
rules.description: All employees who handle, prepare, or serve food must hold a valid Washington State Food Worker Card, renewed every 2 years. Cost: $10 per worker.

rules.title: Handwashing Station
rules.description: A separate, dedicated handwashing sink with hot and cold running water, soap, and paper towels must be accessible at all times during food preparation.

rules.title: Restroom Access
rules.description: Mobile food units operating for more than 4 consecutive hours must provide or arrange access to a public or commercial restroom within 200 feet of the service location.

rules.title: Temperature Control
rules.description: Cold foods must be maintained at 41°F or below; hot foods at 135°F or above. A calibrated food thermometer must be available on-site.

rules.title: Health Permit Application
rules.description: A Food Service Establishment Permit must be obtained before commencing any food service operations. Application fee: $427 for mobile units, $575 for fixed establishments.

rules.title: Annual Health Inspection
rules.description: All food service establishments are subject to unannounced inspections at least once per year. Establishments with critical violations may be inspected quarterly.
